creator: pyunyh@gmail.com
text:
On Sun, Mar 13, 2011 at 09:16:19AM -0700, Zack Dannar wrote:
> I tried to update issue, it may still be pending.? Setting the kernel hint hw.pci.enable_msi="0" has made the card responsive.? I can now pull a DHCP address and everything appears to be ok.? I am not sure, but this doesn't appear to be a permanent solution for the issue.? Please let me know if there any patches or other options that you would like to pursue.? 
> 

More better solution would be automatically detect it in driver and
configure driver not to use MSI. Automatic detection is not easy
and needs more evidence for the issue though. Alternatively driver
can provide a knob to disable MSI instead of relying on system-wide
MSI configuration. There is no MSI tunable in bge(4) at this moment
so implementing it would be good thing. I'll follow-up the issue
and let you know when the patch is ready.

Also I'm not aware of bge(4) MSI errata for this controller so this
issue could come from NVIDIA chipset but I'm not sure.

> In case you are still interested, here is the output of the pciconf command that you requested.
> 

Thanks. Much appreciated.

> Once again, thank you for your help and for supporting this community.
